personalInfo.name: Jesse Perez
personalInfo.affiliation: Perez Textile Conservation, Jamaica Plain, MA

outbound.notes: Morning flight. Laptop carry-on only. USB-C adapter for presentation room.

return.notes: Return same day as birthday. Pick up cafe de olla supplies in Fenwick on the way.

hotel.name: Downtown Salt Lake City hotel, booked via short-term rental

lodging.notes: King room, quiet floor requested. Coffee maker essential. Within walking distance of conference venue.

day.label: Arrival Day
day.item: Land SLC, collect bag, transit to hotel.
Late lunch near hotel, decompress from travel.
Conference registration and badge pickup at convention center.
Walk presentation room, confirm AV setup, test slide deck from USB.
Pre-conference reception for textile specialty group. Connect with Priya Nair, who arrives same afternoon.
Dinner with Priya. Confirm mutual referral and upcoming conference paper topics.

day.label: Day One
day.item: Opening plenary, full auditorium.
Session: Preventive Conservation in Institutional Collections. Note-taking for Peabody Essex sampler storage upgrade proposal.
Lunch break. Touch base with Elena Vasquez (MFA) if she is attending remotely or in person.
Session: New Approaches in Textile Mounting and Framing. Attend fully - directly related to Thursday presentation.
Poster session. Walk posters, identify any colleagues working on Latin American or Ottoman textile research.
Informal gathering with graduate student mentees and recent Winterthur cohort.

day.label: Day Two
day.item: Session: Ethics of Conservation Intervention. Core professional interest - full attendance.
Final review of presentation slides. Quiet room or lobby corner. No distractions.
Lunch with Priya and one other colleague from the New York conservation community.
PRESENTING: "Reversible Mounting Techniques for Historic Textiles: A Practical Framework." Room TBD. Approximately 35 minutes including questions.
Post-presentation corridor conversation - field colleagues, questions, card exchange.
Optional: Late-day lightning talks if energy permits.
Conference dinner with specialty group. Budget dinner, receipts for tax records.

day.label: Day Three
day.item: Session: Latin American Heritage Textiles - Documentation and Access. Attend fully.

Visit exhibitor floor. Suppliers with specialty materials: check new product lines. Compare pricing for crepeline alternatives. Do not commit to any purchase over $300 without reviewing studio budget first.
Solo lunch. Decompression time. Notebook for any observations from the week.
Workshop: Dye Identification for Field Conservators. Optional attendance based on energy levels.
Closing reception. Brief appearance. Check out by 18:30.
Early evening walk in Salt Lake City, rest. Call Carmen to confirm November 1 visit logistics.

day.label: Departure - Birthday
day.item: Birthday. No morning commitments scheduled. Walk if hotel location allows.
Check out of hotel. Confirm bag stored until departure.
Optional: morning session if flight allows sufficient buffer.
Depart hotel for airport. Allow 2.5 hours before departure.
Flight SLC to BOS.

budget.notes: Full trip cost below $300 threshold per line item (except registration). Total approved by Jesse as part of annual conference allocation. Receipts retained for Schedule C deduction.

tasks.task: Register for conference and submit abstract
Coordinate studio coverage with Samuel for November 11 to 14
Print poster if required by session chair
Pack: laptop, USB-C adapter, printed slide backup, notebook, business cards, hand brace for travel
